round2_improvement_notes.linguistic: Agent provided no output in either round; no improvement possible.
round2_improvement_notes.landscape: Acknowledged other agents' findings and synthesized them into the 'kampung' pattern description, but the core evidence and conclusion remained identical to R1.
round2_improvement_notes.botanics: Explicitly referenced Landscape and Meta agents to shift confidence on Indonesia, but the botanical evidence itself was unchanged.
round2_improvement_notes.regulatory: Integrated observations about wall styles from the Meta agent to strengthen the Malaysia case, but the primary regulatory evidence (traffic side, poles) was static.
round2_improvement_notes.meta: Referenced Landscape and Regulatory agents to reinforce the 'kampung' signature, but the visual evidence cited was identical to R1.

role_leakage_notes.botanics: Minor leakage: Referenced 'kampung infrastructure' and 'perimeter wall styles' (Meta/Landscape domains) to support a botanical conclusion.
role_leakage_notes.regulatory: Minor leakage: Referenced 'decorative ventilation blocks' and 'residential wall architecture' (Meta domain) to support a regulatory conclusion.

verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge correctly identified the consensus on Malaysia and utilized the available evidence (Landscape, Botany, Regulatory, Meta) to confirm the prediction. The synthesis is sound but brief, noting the Linguistic agent's lack of output. It lacks a detailed elimination of the Indonesia/Thailand candidates, relying instead on the agents' internal confidence shifts.
